title: [typhlosion] Battle Guide
summary: A [fire]-type special attacker that amplifies its STAB via [flash-fire] and runs [eruption] on a 100 Speed [choice-scarf] frame.
strengths: [flash-fire] absorbs [fire] attacks and boosts STAB damage
100 Speed and 109 Special Attack is a reliable attacker line
[eruption] plus scarf is a powerful revenge-kill combo
weaknesses: Water, Ground, and Rock weaknesses are everywhere in the meta
Thin bulk makes special revenge attacks very dangerous
Pure [fire] coverage is narrow compared to other attackers

recommendedMoves.reason: Base 150 power at full HP — the main [fire] STAB
Fighting coverage for Rock and Dark walls
Water and Ground counter under sun teams
Psychic coverage for Poison and Fighting threats

recommendedItems.reason: Guards the thin bulk on lead duty

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[typhlosion] is a [fire]-type special attacker built around a 100 Speed and 109 Special Attack line that keeps it reliably competitive as a mid-tier cleaner. The hidden ability [flash-fire] is the real draw — it absorbs incoming [fire] attacks and then boosts [typhlosion]'s own [fire] STAB by 50 percent, meaning a single switch into a [fire] move produces one of the hardest-hitting [eruption] attacks. Role-wise [typhlosion] is a classic glass-cannon special attacker with a narrow but reliable plan.

## Strengths
The [flash-fire] interaction is the whole pitch. Absorbing a [fire] attack instead of just resisting it turns every predicted [fire] move into an offensive opportunity. 100 Speed sits at a comfortable tier for scarfed attackers, and the 109 SpA is high enough to pressure switch-ins without extra investment. The coverage options ([focus-blast], [solar-beam], [extrasensory]) fill specific structural holes the [fire] STAB leaves.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The three weaknesses — Water, Ground, and Rock — are among the most common attack types in the format, which means a huge portion of the opposing roster counters [typhlosion]. 78/78/85 defenses don't survive any of those threats, so [typhlosion] has to enter on predicted switches or revenge kills. [eruption] is also HP-scaling, so any chip damage drops the output significantly and the slot loses value over a long game.

## Recommended Moveset
[eruption] is the locked [fire] STAB because its base 150 power at full HP is the reason [typhlosion] is on the team. [focus-blast] covers Rock, Dark, and Steel walls that would otherwise resist the [fire] STAB. [solar-beam] is the sun-team coverage move that answers Water, Ground, and Rock threats in a single slot. [extrasensory] fills the coverage slot outside sun teams and hits Poison and Fighting targets.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[choice-scarf] is the default because locking [typhlosion] into [eruption] as a revenge-killer is exactly the role it plays best. [charcoal] is the alternate for a four-move build that wants flexibility over raw speed. [focus-sash] fits a lead build that demands one guaranteed attack. The ability must be [flash-fire]. Tera [fire] maxes the STAB damage, and Tera [grass] is the defensive option that flips the Water weakness into a resist.

## Teammates & Synergy
A Water and Ground answer is mandatory. A dedicated sun setter boosts [typhlosion]'s [fire] damage and enables [solar-beam] in a single turn, turning the team into a focused offensive unit. A hazard remover preserves [typhlosion]'s HP for [eruption] damage, and a special wall covers the revenge threats that would otherwise remove [typhlosion] before it can sweep.

## Play Patterns
The goal is to keep [typhlosion] at full HP for [eruption]. Enter on predicted [fire] attacks to trigger [flash-fire], then unload [eruption] for boosted damage. Switch out rather than tanking any incidental chip. [focus-blast] is the pick for Rock walls; [extrasensory] for Poison and Fighting. Save the Tera for the moment a Water or Rock move is about to land, and use it defensively to preserve the sweep.
